metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about telling time and reading calendars

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Reading the hour hand on a clock
Days of the week and months of the year

questions.question: On a clock, the short hand is also called the:
questions.explanation: The short hand on a clock is the hour hand. It moves slowly and tells us which hour it is.

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused short hand with long hand
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Knowing which hand is which on a clock
commonMistakes.remediation: Remember: short hand = hour, long hand = minute

questions.question: If the short hand points to the number 3, what hour is it?
questions.explanation: When the short (hour) hand points to 3, it means it is 3 o'clock. The short hand always tells us the hour.

questions.question: How many numbers are there on a clock face?
questions.explanation: A clock face has 12 numbers, from 1 to 12. The short hand goes around the clock twice in one full day (24 hours).

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused 24 hours in a day with numbers on the clock
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Difference between clock face numbers and hours in a day
commonMistakes.remediation: Count the numbers on a real clock face

questions.question: What comes next when counting by 5s? 5, 10, 15, 20, __
questions.explanation: When counting by 5s, you add 5 each time. After 20, the next number is 20 + 5 = 25.

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice counting by 5s using your fingers

questions.question: Count by 5s: 30, 35, 40, __, 50. What number is missing?
questions.explanation: Counting by 5s: 30, 35, 40, 45, 50. The missing number is 45 because 40 + 5 = 45.

questions.question: On a clock, each number stands for 5 minutes. If the long hand points to 6, how many minutes is that?
questions.explanation: Each number on the clock = 5 minutes. The number 6 means 6 x 5 = 30 minutes. So the long hand at 6 means 30 minutes past the hour.

commonMistakes.mistake: Read the number directly without multiplying by 5
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Each clock number represents 5 minutes, not 1 minute

questions.question: How many days are there in one week?
questions.explanation: There are 7 days in one week: Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.

commonMistakes.remediation: Count all 7 days including Saturday and Sunday

questions.question: Which of these is the correct order of months?
questions.explanation: The first three months of the year in order are: January (1st), February (2nd), March (3rd).

questions.question: What number comes between 11 and 1 when you go around a clock?
questions.explanation: On a clock, the numbers go 10, 11, 12, 1, 2... So 12 comes between 11 and 1.

questions.question: If today is Wednesday, what day will it be after 2 days?
questions.explanation: Wednesday + 1 day = Thursday, Thursday + 1 day = Friday. So 2 days after Wednesday is Friday.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counted only 1 day forward instead of 2
commonMistakes.remediation: Use fingers to count each day forward one at a time

excellent.message: Excellent! You know your clock basics and calendar vocabulary very well. You are ready to learn about telling time and reading calendars!

good.message: Good job! You have a solid understanding of clocks and calendars. Let's start learning about telling time and calendars!

partial.message: You know some things about clocks and calendars but need to review a few basics. Watch the review videos first and then we will learn together!

needs_review.message: Let's first review how to read the clock hands, count by 5s, and learn the days and months. We have some fun videos to help you get ready!

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We'll show you some fun videos about clocks, counting by 5s, and the calendar first.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You're all set to learn about telling time and reading calendars!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try again to check your progress.
